findings.description: ShadowStateDB access safe — uses config.shadow.shadows_db_path (validated config path), calls init_schema() to create tables. ShadowStateDB is separately audited (pica-security-shadow_state.json). No raw SQL in this module.

Credential exposure — init_gateway(config.deepseek_api_key, config.deepseek_base_url) passes API key to gateway layer. Same pattern as before extraction. Key stays in memory, never logged, never written to disk. No new exposure path.

Graceful degradation for missing modules — flash_triage ImportError falls back to flash_preprocessor (line 123-129), investigation_loop ImportError skips Pro HVR with log message (line 163-164). Pipeline continues with reduced functionality rather than crashing.

Archive operations wrapped in try/except that logs and continues — _archive() helper (line 45-51) silently handles all exceptions. Archive failures never crash the pipeline. No sensitive data in archive payloads (article metadata, classification counts, hypothesis summaries only).
No user input processed — all data flows from config (MarketMindConfig, validated upstream) and LLM pipeline stages. No CLI interaction, no file upload, no URL parameter injection surface.
datetime.now(timezone.utc) used for session_date — timezone-aware, compliant with root CLAUDE.md §3.2 time-awareness rule.
Optional Phase F features (BackgroundScheduler, MultimodalAdapter) gated behind config flags (scheduler_enabled, gemini_flash_enabled) — disabled by default, no risk from dormant code paths.
File at 188 lines — under the 250-line Python module soft threshold. Clean single-responsibility: runs pipeline stages 0-3 only. No extraction needed.

notes: Extracted pre-Gate 1 module with safe credential passing (same pattern as original), two-level graceful degradation for optional modules, and archived data containing only non-sensitive pipeline metadata. No user input surface. All database ops delegated to separately audited modules. Phase F features gated behind disabled-by-default flags.
